name: Tokugawa Ieyasu
description: Tokugawa Ieyasu (1543-1616) was the founder and first shogun of the Tokugawa shogunate of Japan, which effectively ruled Japan from the Battle of Sekigahara in 1600 until the Meiji Restoration in 1868. Ieyasu was a powerful daimyo and initially an ally of Oda Nobunaga and Toyotomi Hideyoshi. After Hideyoshi's death, Ieyasu maneuvered himself into a position of power, ultimately defeating his rivals in the Battle of Sekigahara. He established the Tokugawa shogunate in Edo (modern Tokyo), ushering in a period of relative peace and stability known as the Edo period. Ieyasu is known for his strategic brilliance, political acumen, and his ability to consolidate power after decades of warfare. His legacy includes the establishment of a rigid social hierarchy, a centralized government, and a flourishing economy. He is revered as one of the great unifiers of Japan and a symbol of strength, resilience, and political prowess. His policies shaped Japanese society for over 250 years, leaving an indelible mark on the nation's history and culture. He navigated treacherous political landscapes, surviving betrayals and conflicts to forge a lasting dynasty. He is a figure of immense importance in Japanese history, representing the end of the Sengoku period and the beginning of a new era of peace and stability.
subtitle: Founder of the Tokugawa Shogunate
achievements: Creation of Edo as the Shogunate capital

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Sekigahara
keyMyths.description: The Battle of Sekigahara was a decisive battle that paved the way for Tokugawa Ieyasu's rise to power. His victory secured control over Japan and led to the establishment of the Tokugawa Shogunate.

keyMyths.title: Ieyasu's Patience
keyMyths.description: Ieyasu was known for his patience and strategic thinking, often waiting for the opportune moment to strike. This approach allowed him to outmaneuver his enemies and consolidate his power over time.

keyMyths.title: The Tokugawa Code
keyMyths.description: Ieyasu established a strict code of conduct and laws that governed the samurai class and the general population. These rules helped to maintain social order and stability during the Edo period.

sources.author: Stephen Turnbull
sources.work: Samurai: The World of the Warrior
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of samurai culture and history.
sources.text: Comprehensive overview of samurai culture and history.

sources.author: Conrad Totman
sources.description: General history of Japan, including the Tokugawa period.
sources.text: General history of Japan, including the Tokugawa period.
